title: Creamy Uruguayan Sweet Potato Stew
description: A cultured and indulgent Uruguayan dish featuring sweet potatoes in a creamy sauce, made with vegetable oil and deglazed for added depth of flavor, and is macrobiotic friendly.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in an earthenware bowl over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the diced sweet potatoes, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 5 minutes.
Deglaze the earthenware bowl with the white wine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Add the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the sweet potatoes are tender.
Stir in the coconut cream and continue to simmer for an additional 5 minutes.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
